prose: The positive call rests on curated CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence, a diagnostic anchor that grounds this evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis [E1]. Mechanistically, the molecular initiating event is plausibly localized to MitoCarta3.0 Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the proposed adverse outcome expressed in the Kamath 2022 SOX6/AGTR1-defined vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population—a cell-type grounding consistent with an AOP-linked mitochondrial trajectory [E2]. Assay data offer only weak corroboration here: 6-hydroxydopamine is thin in ToxCast, behaving largely as a research reagent, so bioactivity is anti-diagnostic and cannot bear the call [E3]. Brain-exposure modeling flags the compound as non-penetrant by structure, consistent with its established use as a directly delivered lesioning agent that enters neurons via transporters rather than by passive diffusion [E4]. This does not weaken the curated mechanism; it clarifies the route. On balance, the curated-anchored-only tier is appropriately reserved, reflecting reliance on [E1] with mechanistic plausibility from [E2].

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
Non-penetrant by structure (e.g. lesioning agent delivered directly / enters via transporters).
